site.title: Digital Security Checklists for Activists
site.description: Plain language steps for digital security. Because protecting yourself helps keep your whole community safer.

essentials.title: Security essentials
essentials.description: Core security practices every activist should follow.

signal.title: Signal security
signal.description: Secure your Signal messaging app for safer communications.

protest.title: Prepare for a protest
protest.description: Digital security for anyone attending a protest.

ice.title: ICE watch digital security
ice.description: Digital security for ICE watch and rapid response networks.

doxxing.title: Doxxing defense
doxxing.description: Protect yourself from doxxing and online harassment.

travel.title: Travel & flight security
travel.description: Digital security practices for activists traveling.

emergency.title: Emergency planning
emergency.description: Establish an emergency support network in case you're detained or threatened.

secondary.title: Secondary phone
secondary.description: Set up an extra phone for activism and protests.

hero.title: Digital Security Checklists for Activists
hero.description: Plain language steps for digital security. Because protecting yourself helps keep your whole community safer.

trustPoints.experienceDescription: Our guides draw from real organizing work and direct feedback from activists. While every situation is unique, we aim to share practical insights that can help inform your decisions.
trustPoints.updatedDescription: Our content is regularly reviewed by organizers who understand security concerns. We work to keep information current as risks and technologies evolve.

misconceptions.sectionDescription: When we start talking about digital security, we often hear these concerns. We think there's more to the story.
misconceptions.nothingToHideDescription: The more engaged you are in any social movement work, the more your data can be used to map networks of activists. The best time to start protecting yourself is before you're as deeply involved.
misconceptions.alreadyKnowDescription: While data collection is widespread, you can significantly reduce your digital footprint and protect future activities.
misconceptions.dontCareTitle: "They don't care about what I'm doing"
misconceptions.dontCareDescription: Mass surveillance affects everyone. Your data can be used to profile communities, predict protests, or target those you care about. Protecting your communications helps protect all of us.

homepage.trustHeading: Simple guides to keep us more safe
homepage.trustDescription: We built this because digital security shouldn't be overwhelming. We take a harm reduction approach: start where you are and do what you can.

translationFallback.message: This page is not yet available in your language. You are viewing the English version.
translationFallback.suggest: You can use your browser's built-in translation feature to translate this page.

pageNotices.translationUnreviewed: This page was automatically translated. Only {approvalPercent}% of this page is human-reviewed so far. Other text may have errors. <link>Help us review this translation</link>.

branding.title: Activist Checklist

footer.disclaimer: Heads up: We provide info and resources, not legal advice. Consult an attorney for your specific situation.

riskLevel.everyoneDefault: This section is for anyone doing activism or advocacy work.
riskLevel.mediumDefault: This section is for you if you are in a leadership role or you are doing activism that is more likely be targetted by the state or your opposition.
riskLevel.highDefault: This section is for you are in a high-profile role or your activism involves high-risk work that could result in serious consequences or retaliation.
riskLevel.forYouIf: This section is for you if:

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.intro: Your feedback helps make this resource more useful and secure for everyone. We welcome suggestions for:
contact.suggestion1: Correcting any inaccuracies or outdated information
contact.suggestion2: Making our guides clearer and easier to understand
contact.suggestion4: Suggesting new topics we should cover
contact.successMessage: Message sent successfully! We'll get back to you soon.
contact.githubDescription: If you're okay with sharing publicly, you can submit feedback, questions, and feature requests through our GitHub repository.
contact.emailDescription: Using Proton Mail? Your messages will be automatically encrypted. For other providers, you can use our PGP key. Or you can just email this address unencrypted.

contactForm.emailNote: Note on email replies: Unless you use Proton Mail, your email provider can read any replies we send to your message.
contactForm.signalUsernameHelp: Your username is different than your display name. Find your username at Signal > User Icon (top right) > Settings. It will be listed near the top, just underneath your phone number.
contactForm.privacyText: Your message is securely encrypted and sent to our Proton Mail account. We don't collect any personal information about you.
contactForm.errorMessage: Failed to send message. Please try again later.
contactForm.responseNone: No response needed (your message remains anonymous)

feedbackCta.heading: Have Questions?
feedbackCta.description: Let us know if you have questions or feedback so we can make these guides as useful as possible.
feedbackCta.successMessage: Thanks for your feedback! We'll review it and use it to improve the guides. If you're finding the site valuable, please help spread the word!

relatedGuides.headingBlock: Keep learning with these related guides
relatedGuides.headingFooter: If you found this helpful, also check out these guides

news.description: Latest news about state surveillance and threats facing social movements.
news.tipInvitation: Have news you think should be included?
news.tipSuffix: and help keep our community informed.

changelog.title: Recent site updates
changelog.description: A history of changes to checklists, pages, and resources on the site.

updates.metaTitle: Is your device still receiving security updates? | Activist Checklist
updates.metaDescription: Check whether your phone, tablet, watch, or laptop is still getting security updates. Out-of-date devices accumulate publicly known vulnerabilities and are easier to hack if it is seized or targeted.
updates.title: Is your device still receiving security updates?
updates.intro: If your device isn't able to receive security updates, it makes it easier for the cops (and hackers) to get into it and access all your data.
updates.searchHelp: Need help finding it? Select your operating system above for guidance.
updates.searchAriaLabel: Search for your device or operating system
updates.noMatchesHelp: Try the manufacturer name (e.g. "Galaxy A14" instead of just "A14").
updates.noMatchesSupported: We currently track Apple, Google, Samsung, Motorola, OnePlus, Nokia, plus iOS, iPadOS, macOS, Android, and Windows.
updates.noMatchesWindowsLaptop: Windows laptop manufacturers don't announce when they stop offering security updates, so checking the laptop model won't tell you. Check your Windows version instead. That's what determines whether you're still receiving updates.
updates.noMatchesWindowsPathLabel: How to check your Windows version:
updates.loadError: We couldn't load the device list. <retryLink>Try again</retryLink> or check <link>endoflife.date</link> directly.
updates.snapshotStaleBanner: Our device list may be out of date. Last updated {date}. <link>Check endoflife.date directly</link>

findYourModel.label: How to find your device model:
findYourModel.labelWindows: How to check your Windows version:
findYourModel.appleWatch: Watch app on iPhone → My Watch → General → About → Model
findYourModel.mac: Apple menu → About This Mac → Overview
findYourModel.windows: Press Windows key + R, type winver, then Enter
findYourModel.windowsCaveat: Windows laptop manufacturers don't announce when they stop offering security updates, so checking the laptop model won't tell you. Check your Windows version instead. That's what determines whether you're still receiving updates.

otherInfo.title: Other devices
otherInfo.body: We currently track Apple, Google, Samsung, Motorola, OnePlus, and Nokia devices, plus iOS, iPadOS, macOS, Android, and Windows. For anything else, <link>search online</link> for "is [DEVICE MODEL] still receiving security updates."

settingsPath.ios: Settings → General → Software Update → Update Now
settingsPath.ipados: Settings → General → Software Update → Update Now
settingsPath.macos: Apple menu → System Settings → General → Software Update → Update Now
settingsPath.android: Settings → System → Software update → Check for updates
settingsPath.windows: Settings → Windows Update → Check for updates
settingsPath.watchos: Watch app on your iPhone → My Watch → General → Software Update → Install
settingsPath.oneplus: Settings → System → System updates → Check for updates

osVersionPath.windows: Press Windows key + R, type winver, then Enter
osVersionPath.watchos: Watch app on your iPhone → My Watch → General → About → Version

result.deviceUnsupportedTitle: {label} is no longer receiving security updates.
result.deviceUnsupportedSubtitleUnmaintained: The manufacturer has stopped issuing security updates.
result.deviceUnsupportedSubtitleAge: Released {age}. No published end-of-support date and the manufacturer doesn't appear to be issuing updates.
result.ageYearsAgo: {years, plural, =0 {this year} =1 {1 year ago} other {# years ago}}
result.deviceUncertainTitle: We can't confirm whether {label} is still receiving updates.
result.deviceUncertainSubtitle: Released {age}. The manufacturer hasn't published an end-of-support date, and devices this old may or may not still be receiving updates.
result.osEoasNote: The vendor has moved this version to security-only updates. Plan an upgrade when convenient.
result.osUnsupportedTitle: {label} is no longer receiving security updates.
result.osCheckDeviceHint: Or enter your device model in the box above to see if it is still able to receive security updates.
result.latestOsReminderTitle: Make sure you're up to date
result.latestOsReminderFamilyLatest: Make sure you're running the latest {os} (currently {version}).
result.latestOsReminderGeneric: Make sure your device is fully updated.
result.deviceMaxOsWarningTitle: A note about your device's OS
result.deviceMaxOsWarning: Your {label} only supports up to {os} {maxVersion}. {os} {maxVersion} will lose security updates before {os} {latestVersion} does. Newer devices will keep receiving updates longer.
result.deviceMaxOsWarningWithDate: Your {label} only supports up to {os} {maxVersion}, which is expected to lose security updates around {eolDate}. Newer devices that can run {os} {latestVersion} will keep receiving updates longer.
result.deviceMaxOsEolWarning: Your {label} tops out at {os} {maxVersion}, which has officially ended support. You may still receive occasional updates, but treat this device as low-trust for sensitive work and plan to replace it.
result.deviceUpgradePlan: If long-term security matters for your work, plan a hardware upgrade.
result.threatModel: Every week without an update, more ways to break into your device become public knowledge. If the cops seize your phone at a protest or a border crossing (or if someone targets you with <spywareLink>spyware</spywareLink>) those known weaknesses are what they'll use to get inside and read everything on it (even your undeleted Signal messages.
result.threatModelSoft: Until you confirm, treat this device as potentially out-of-date for sensitive work.

eolSoon.titleMonths: {label} will lose security updates in about {months, plural, =1 {1 month} other {# months}}.
eolSoon.action: Start planning a replacement now. Once updates stop, your device begins accumulating publicly known vulnerabilities.

buyingGuidance.heading: When you replace it
buyingGuidance.yearsPattern: Most {deviceLabel} get security updates for {min}-{max} years from their release date.
buyingGuidance.checkBeforeBuying: If you're buying used, search for the model here first to see how long it'll keep getting updates.

ctaReplace.phone: We suggest getting a new phone.
ctaReplace.tablet: We suggest getting a new tablet.
ctaReplace.laptop: We suggest getting a new laptop.
ctaReplace.desktop: We suggest getting a new Mac.
ctaReplace.watch: We suggest getting a new watch.
ctaReplace.generic: We suggest getting a new device.

osCheckStep.headingForOs: Which version of {os} are you running?
osCheckStep.headingGeneric: Which version is your device running?
osCheckStep.subheadingHelp: Open it on your device to check:

osNeedsUpdate.heading: Your operating system needs an update
osNeedsUpdate.headingMaybe: Your operating system might need an update
osNeedsUpdate.body: Update to the latest version to get the most recent security updates.
osNeedsUpdate.noUpdatesAvailableButton: No updates available and I'm on a version older than {os} {major}
osNeedsUpdate.noUpdatesAvailableButtonCodename: No updates available and I'm on a version older than {os} {major} ({codename})

result.deviceUnsupportedSubtitleStuckOnOs: You can't get newer security updates on this device, so it has stopped receiving the patches that protect your data.

finalSuccess.heading: You're up to date.
finalSuccess.osCheckNoVersion: Operating system is up to date.

result.windowsUpdateHelp: Open Settings → Windows Update and click 'Check for updates'.
